description: An introduction to the fundamental technologies of the web. Students will learn to build structured, styled, and interactive web pages using HTML5, CSS3, and vanilla JavaScript. Emphasis is placed on semantic markup, responsive design, and DOM manipulation.
objectives: Understand the structure and semantics of HTML5
Design responsive layouts using CSS3 Flexbox and Grid
Manipulate the DOM using JavaScript
Handle user events and form validation
Deploy a static website

chapters.title: HTML5 Foundations
chapters.description: Document structure, semantic tags, forms, tables, multimedia elements.

chapters.title: CSS3 Styling & Layout
chapters.description: Selectors, box model, Flexbox, CSS Grid, media queries and responsive design.

chapters.title: JavaScript Basics
chapters.description: Variables, types, control flow, functions, arrays and objects.

chapters.title: DOM Manipulation
chapters.description: Selecting elements, modifying content, handling events, dynamic updates.

chapters.title: Forms & Validation
chapters.description: Client-side form validation, input types, regex patterns.

chapters.title: Mini-Project
chapters.description: Build and deploy a complete responsive multi-page website.

description: This course introduces programming through Python, one of the most widely used languages in industry and research. Students will develop strong problem-solving skills and get hands-on with Python's readable syntax, standard library, and key programming paradigms.
objectives: Master Python syntax, data types, and control structures
Write reusable functions and work with modules
Manipulate files, strings, and collections
Apply object-oriented programming basics
Solve algorithmic problems with Python

chapters.title: Getting Started with Python
chapters.description: Installation, REPL, variables, types, operators and basic I/O.

chapters.title: Control Flow
chapters.description: Conditionals, loops (for/while), break, continue, and comprehensions.

chapters.title: Functions & Scope
chapters.description: Defining functions, parameters, return values, recursion, lambda.

chapters.title: Data Structures
chapters.description: Lists, tuples, dictionaries, sets — operations and use cases.

chapters.title: File I/O & Exceptions
chapters.description: Reading and writing files, exception handling with try/except.

chapters.title: Intro to OOP
chapters.description: Classes, objects, attributes, methods, inheritance basics.

chapters.title: Standard Library & Modules
chapters.description: math, random, datetime, os — practical usage of built-in modules.

description: A rigorous introduction to the design and analysis of fundamental data structures and algorithms. Students learn how to organize data efficiently and select appropriate algorithms for sorting, searching, and graph traversal problems.
objectives: Understand and implement core data structures: arrays, linked lists, stacks, queues, trees
Analyze algorithm complexity using Big-O notation
Implement and compare sorting algorithms
Traverse and manipulate trees and graphs
Choose the right data structure for a given problem

chapters.title: Arrays and Linked Lists
chapters.description: Static vs dynamic arrays, singly and doubly linked lists, insertion and deletion.

chapters.title: Stacks & Queues
chapters.description: LIFO/FIFO principles, implementations, practical applications.

chapters.title: Sorting Algorithms
chapters.description: Bubble, selection, insertion, merge sort, quicksort — correctness and complexity.

chapters.title: Searching Algorithms
chapters.description: Linear search, binary search, hash tables.

chapters.title: Trees
chapters.description: Binary trees, BST operations (insert, delete, search), traversals (inorder, preorder, postorder).

chapters.title: Introduction to Graphs
chapters.description: Representations (adjacency matrix/list), BFS, DFS.

description: An introductory course on algorithmic thinking. Students learn to break down problems, design step-by-step solutions, and express them through pseudocode and flowcharts. This course lays the conceptual foundation for all future programming courses.
objectives: Develop structured problem-solving habits
Write correct and readable pseudocode
Design algorithms using sequence, selection, and iteration
Understand the concept of correctness and termination
Implement basic algorithms in a high-level language

chapters.title: What is an Algorithm?
chapters.description: Definition, properties, real-world examples, problem decomposition.

chapters.title: Pseudocode & Flowcharts
chapters.description: Notation, conventions, translating problems to pseudocode.

chapters.title: Sequence and Selection
chapters.description: If/else structures, nested conditions, decision trees.

chapters.title: Iteration
chapters.description: For loops, while loops, loop invariants.

chapters.title: Arrays & Simple Structures
chapters.description: Sequential access, search, min/max, sum and average.

chapters.title: Introduction to Functions
chapters.description: Modularizing algorithms, parameters and return values.

description: This course introduces the theoretical foundations of computational complexity. Students study how to formally measure and classify the efficiency of algorithms, exploring concepts such as time complexity, space complexity, and complexity classes.
objectives: Define and apply Big-O, Big-Omega, and Big-Theta notations
Analyze time and space complexity of recursive and iterative algorithms
Understand P vs NP and why it matters
Identify NP-complete problems and reduction techniques
Evaluate algorithm trade-offs in practical scenarios

chapters.title: Asymptotic Notation
chapters.description: Big-O, Omega, Theta — formal definitions and practical analysis.

chapters.title: Recurrence Relations
chapters.description: Solving recurrences with substitution, recursion trees, and the Master Theorem.

chapters.title: Sorting Lower Bounds
chapters.description: Decision tree model, Omega(n log n) lower bound for comparison-based sorting.

chapters.title: Complexity Classes
chapters.description: P, NP, NP-hard, NP-complete — definitions and significance.

chapters.title: Reductions
chapters.description: Polynomial-time reductions, proving NP-completeness by reduction.

chapters.title: Practical Implications
chapters.description: Heuristics, approximation algorithms, when exact solutions are infeasible.

description: A continuation of Data Structures I, this course dives deeper into advanced structures and algorithm design strategies. Students will explore heaps, balanced trees, graph algorithms, and paradigms such as dynamic programming and greedy algorithms.
objectives: Implement and analyze advanced data structures (heaps, AVL trees, hash maps)
Apply greedy algorithms to optimization problems
Use dynamic programming to solve complex subproblems
Implement shortest-path and minimum spanning tree algorithms
Compare algorithmic strategies and justify choices

chapters.title: Heaps & Priority Queues
chapters.description: Min/max heaps, heapify, heap sort, priority queue applications.

chapters.title: Balanced Trees
chapters.description: AVL trees, rotations, B-trees introduction.

chapters.title: Hash Tables
chapters.description: Hash functions, collision resolution (chaining, open addressing), load factor.

chapters.title: Greedy Algorithms
chapters.description: Activity selection, Huffman encoding, interval scheduling.

chapters.title: Dynamic Programming
chapters.description: Memoization, tabulation, classic problems: Fibonacci, knapsack, LCS.

chapters.title: Graph Algorithms
chapters.description: Dijkstra, Bellman-Ford, Prim, Kruskal — correctness and complexity.

description: A practical introduction to the Linux operating system environment. Students will become comfortable with the command line, shell scripting, file system management, process control, and system administration basics — essential skills for any software engineer.
objectives: Navigate and manage the Linux file system
Write effective Bash shell scripts
Manage processes, users, and permissions
Configure basic networking in Linux
Automate repetitive tasks with cron and scripting

chapters.title: Linux Fundamentals
chapters.description: History, distributions, terminal basics, man pages, file system hierarchy.

chapters.title: File System Management
chapters.description: ls, cd, cp, mv, rm, find, permissions (chmod, chown), symbolic links.

chapters.title: Text Processing
chapters.description: grep, sed, awk, cut, sort, pipes and redirection.

chapters.title: Shell Scripting
chapters.description: Variables, conditions, loops, functions, script arguments in Bash.

chapters.title: Process Management
chapters.description: ps, top, kill, fg/bg, jobs, signals.

chapters.title: Users & Permissions
chapters.description: User management, groups, sudo, file permissions model.

chapters.title: Networking & Automation
chapters.description: ifconfig, ssh, scp, cron jobs, system monitoring.

description: This course introduces the fundamental principles of computer networking, with a focus on network architecture, the OSI and TCP/IP models, and how data is transmitted across networks. Students will gain both theoretical understanding and practical skills in network configuration.
objectives: Understand the OSI and TCP/IP reference models
Explain how data is encapsulated and routed across networks
Configure basic LAN and WAN setups
Understand IP addressing, subnetting, and CIDR notation
Analyze network traffic using tools like Wireshark

chapters.title: Introduction to Networking
chapters.description: Network types (LAN, WAN, MAN), topologies, transmission media.

chapters.title: OSI Model
chapters.description: Seven layers, role of each layer, encapsulation and decapsulation.

chapters.title: TCP/IP Stack
chapters.description: IP, TCP, UDP — addressing, port numbers, handshake.

chapters.title: IP Addressing & Subnetting
chapters.description: IPv4 classes, subnet masks, CIDR, NAT.

chapters.title: Network Devices
chapters.description: Hubs, switches, routers, access points — roles and comparison.

chapters.title: Routing Fundamentals
chapters.description: Static vs dynamic routing, routing tables, RIP, OSPF basics.

chapters.title: Practical Lab
chapters.description: Packet Tracer simulations, Wireshark traffic analysis.

description: An introduction to relational databases: how data is modeled, organized, stored, and queried. Students learn to design database schemas, write SQL queries, and understand the fundamental principles behind database management systems.
objectives: Model real-world data using Entity-Relationship diagrams
Design normalized relational schemas
Write queries in SQL (SELECT, JOIN, subqueries, aggregations)
Understand transactions and ACID properties
Use a real DBMS (PostgreSQL or MySQL)

chapters.title: Introduction to Databases
chapters.description: Database concepts, DBMS overview, relational model, advantages over file systems.

chapters.title: Entity-Relationship Modeling
chapters.description: Entities, attributes, relationships, cardinality, ER diagrams.

chapters.title: Relational Schema Design
chapters.description: Tables, primary/foreign keys, constraints, normalization (1NF–3NF).

chapters.title: SQL Fundamentals
chapters.description: DDL (CREATE, ALTER, DROP), DML (INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE), SELECT basics.

chapters.title: Advanced SQL
chapters.description: JOINs, subqueries, GROUP BY, HAVING, window functions.

chapters.title: Transactions & Integrity
chapters.description: ACID properties, BEGIN/COMMIT/ROLLBACK, integrity constraints.

description: A deep dive into Object-Oriented Programming using Java. Students will master core OOP principles — encapsulation, inheritance, polymorphism, and abstraction — and learn to build well-structured, maintainable Java applications.
objectives: Understand and apply the four pillars of OOP
Design class hierarchies using inheritance and interfaces
Handle exceptions and manage resources properly
Use the Java Collections Framework effectively
Write unit tests with JUnit

chapters.title: Java Fundamentals
chapters.description: JVM, syntax, types, operators, control flow — compared to Python.

chapters.title: Classes & Objects
chapters.description: Fields, constructors, methods, access modifiers, this keyword.

chapters.title: Encapsulation & Abstraction
chapters.description: Getters/setters, abstract classes, interfaces.

chapters.title: Inheritance & Polymorphism
chapters.description: Extends, super, method overriding, dynamic dispatch.

chapters.title: Exception Handling
chapters.description: Checked vs unchecked exceptions, try/catch/finally, custom exceptions.

chapters.title: Collections Framework
chapters.description: ArrayList, LinkedList, HashMap, HashSet, iterators, generics introduction.

chapters.title: Testing with JUnit
chapters.description: Unit test basics, assertions, test-driven development principles.

description: An advanced Java course covering modern programming paradigms beyond basic OOP. Students explore generic programming, functional-style Java with lambdas and streams, and concurrent programming with threads, executors, and synchronization primitives.
objectives: Write type-safe code using Java Generics
Apply functional programming with lambda expressions and the Stream API
Understand concurrency concepts: threads, synchronization, race conditions
Use the java.util.concurrent framework effectively
Design thread-safe data structures and services

chapters.title: Generics
chapters.description: Generic classes, methods, bounded type parameters, wildcards, type erasure.

chapters.title: Functional Interfaces & Lambdas
chapters.description: Function, Predicate, Consumer, Supplier — lambda syntax and method references.

chapters.title: Stream API
chapters.description: Creating streams, intermediate and terminal operations, parallel streams.

chapters.title: Optional & Modern Java Features
chapters.description: Optional class, records, sealed classes, pattern matching (Java 17+).

chapters.title: Threads & Concurrency Basics
chapters.description: Thread lifecycle, Runnable, race conditions, synchronized keyword.

chapters.title: java.util.concurrent
chapters.description: ExecutorService, Future, CompletableFuture, concurrent collections.

chapters.title: Concurrency Patterns
chapters.description: Producer-consumer, thread pools, deadlock detection and avoidance.

description: An accessible first course in machine learning. Students will understand the fundamental concepts behind learning from data, implement key algorithms, and evaluate models using real-world datasets — without requiring deep mathematical prerequisites.
objectives: Understand the machine learning workflow end-to-end
Implement and evaluate supervised learning models
Apply unsupervised techniques for clustering and dimensionality reduction
Prevent overfitting using regularization and cross-validation
Use scikit-learn and pandas for practical ML pipelines

chapters.title: Introduction to ML
chapters.description: Supervised, unsupervised, reinforcement learning — use cases and landscape.

chapters.title: Data Preparation
chapters.description: Pandas, missing values, encoding, normalization, train/test split.

chapters.title: Linear Models
chapters.description: Linear regression, logistic regression, gradient descent.

chapters.title: Decision Trees & Ensembles
chapters.description: Decision trees, random forests, bagging, boosting.

chapters.title: Unsupervised Learning
chapters.description: K-means clustering, hierarchical clustering, PCA.

chapters.title: Model Evaluation
chapters.description: Accuracy, precision, recall, F1, ROC-AUC, cross-validation.

chapters.title: Intro to Neural Networks
chapters.description: Perceptron, backpropagation, intro to deep learning with Keras.

description: A practical course on modern JavaScript frontend development using popular frameworks. Students build real-world single-page applications, learn component-based architecture, manage state, consume REST APIs, and understand the modern frontend ecosystem.
objectives: Understand the component-based architecture pattern
Build SPAs using React (or Vue)
Manage application state with hooks and context
Consume REST APIs asynchronously
Set up a modern frontend toolchain (Vite, npm, ESLint)
prerequisites: OOP with Java or Python (OOP concepts required)

chapters.title: Modern JavaScript (ES6+)
chapters.description: Arrow functions, destructuring, spread, modules, async/await, Promises.

chapters.title: Introduction to React
chapters.description: JSX, components, props, rendering lists, conditional rendering.

chapters.title: State & Hooks
chapters.description: useState, useEffect, useRef, custom hooks.

chapters.title: Routing
chapters.description: React Router — declarative routing, dynamic segments, navigation.

chapters.title: API Integration
chapters.description: fetch, axios, loading states, error handling, REST conventions.

chapters.title: State Management
chapters.description: Context API, introduction to Redux / Zustand.

chapters.title: Final Project
chapters.description: Full SPA: auth, routing, API calls, deployment on Vercel/Netlify.

description: A course on software design using Object-Oriented Analysis and Design (OOAD) principles and the Unified Modeling Language (UML). Students learn to model, design, and document complex software systems before writing a single line of code.
objectives: Capture requirements using use case diagrams and user stories
Model system structure with class and object diagrams
Represent dynamic behavior with sequence and state machine diagrams
Apply SOLID principles to guide OO design
Recognize and apply key design patterns (GoF)

chapters.title: Introduction to OOAD
chapters.description: Software design process, the role of modeling, UML overview.

chapters.title: Requirements & Use Cases
chapters.description: Use case diagrams, actors, include/extend relationships.

chapters.title: Class & Object Diagrams
chapters.description: Classes, attributes, methods, associations, aggregation, composition, inheritance.

chapters.title: Sequence & Communication Diagrams
chapters.description: Modeling interactions between objects over time.

chapters.title: State & Activity Diagrams
chapters.description: State machines, transitions, guards, activity flows.

chapters.title: SOLID Principles
chapters.description: Single responsibility, Open/closed, Liskov, Interface segregation, Dependency inversion.

chapters.title: Design Patterns
chapters.description: Creational (Singleton, Factory), Structural (Adapter, Decorator), Behavioral (Observer, Strategy).

description: A deep dive into the protocols and applications that power modern networks. Students will study the TCP/IP stack in detail, explore key application-layer protocols (HTTP, DNS, SMTP), and implement networked programs using sockets.
objectives: Understand TCP/IP protocol stack layers in detail
Analyze HTTP, DNS, DHCP, FTP, SMTP, and TLS
Program client-server applications using sockets
Understand how the web works end-to-end
Capture and analyze real network traffic with Wireshark

chapters.title: Transport Layer: TCP & UDP
chapters.description: Reliable transmission, flow control, congestion control, three-way handshake.

chapters.title: Application Layer Protocols
chapters.description: HTTP/1.1, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, request/response cycle, headers and status codes.

chapters.title: DNS
chapters.description: Name resolution, recursive vs iterative queries, DNS records, TTL.

chapters.title: Email Protocols
chapters.description: SMTP, IMAP, POP3 — how email traverses the internet.

chapters.title: Socket Programming
chapters.description: TCP and UDP sockets in Python/Java — building a simple chat server.

chapters.title: Security in Protocols
chapters.description: TLS/SSL handshake, certificates, HTTPS, common vulnerabilities.

chapters.title: Network Lab
chapters.description: Wireshark captures, protocol analysis, troubleshooting scenarios.

description: A combined course covering both computer architecture and operating systems. Students learn how computers work at the hardware level and how operating systems manage hardware resources to provide services to applications.
objectives: Understand CPU architecture, instruction sets, and memory hierarchy
Explain how operating systems manage processes, memory, and I/O
Understand virtual memory, paging, and segmentation
Analyze deadlock conditions and scheduling algorithms
Work with system calls and low-level programming in C

chapters.title: Computer Organization
chapters.description: Von Neumann architecture, ALU, registers, bus, memory types.

chapters.title: Instruction Set Architecture
chapters.description: RISC vs CISC, assembly basics, instruction encoding.

chapters.title: Memory Hierarchy
chapters.description: Cache levels, locality, RAM, virtual memory principles.

chapters.title: OS Overview & Process Management
chapters.description: Kernel vs user space, process lifecycle, PCB, context switching.

chapters.title: CPU Scheduling
chapters.description: FCFS, SJF, Round Robin, priority scheduling, multilevel queues.

chapters.title: Memory Management
chapters.description: Paging, segmentation, page tables, TLB, page replacement algorithms.

chapters.title: Concurrency & Deadlocks
chapters.description: Mutual exclusion, semaphores, monitors, deadlock detection/avoidance.

chapters.title: File Systems & I/O
chapters.description: File system structure, inodes, I/O scheduling, device drivers.

description: A broad introduction to the field of cybersecurity. Students will understand the threat landscape, core security principles, common attack vectors, and foundational defense mechanisms. This course builds the security mindset every engineer needs.
objectives: Understand the CIA triad and core security principles
Identify and categorize common cyber threats and vulnerabilities
Explain how cryptographic primitives protect data
Apply basic web and network security techniques
Understand legal, ethical, and compliance frameworks

chapters.title: Security Fundamentals
chapters.description: CIA triad, threat models, attack surfaces, security mindset.

chapters.title: Cryptography Basics
chapters.description: Symmetric encryption (AES), asymmetric (RSA), hashing (SHA), digital signatures.

chapters.title: Network Security
chapters.description: Firewalls, IDS/IPS, VPNs, network scanning, packet analysis.

chapters.title: Web Application Security
chapters.description: OWASP Top 10: SQL injection, XSS, CSRF, broken authentication.

chapters.title: System & OS Security
chapters.description: Privilege escalation, hardening, patch management, logging.

chapters.title: Social Engineering
chapters.description: Phishing, pretexting, baiting, security awareness.

chapters.title: Ethics, Law & Compliance
chapters.description: GDPR, responsible disclosure, penetration testing ethics, incident response.

description: An advanced database course focusing on security, automation, and administration. Students will learn to protect databases from threats, automate routine operations using stored procedures and triggers, and manage database performance and replication.
objectives: Implement role-based access control in a DBMS
Write stored procedures, functions, and triggers
Understand and apply transaction isolation levels
Set up automated backups and replication
Audit database activity and detect anomalies

chapters.title: Database Security Model
chapters.description: Users, roles, privileges, GRANT/REVOKE, row-level security.

chapters.title: Stored Procedures & Functions
chapters.description: PL/SQL or PL/pgSQL syntax, parameters, cursors, exception handling.

chapters.title: Triggers
chapters.description: BEFORE/AFTER triggers, row vs statement level, use cases and pitfalls.

chapters.title: Transactions & Isolation
chapters.description: ACID revisited, isolation levels (READ COMMITTED, SERIALIZABLE), locking.

chapters.title: Backup & Recovery
chapters.description: Full and incremental backups, point-in-time recovery, pg_dump, mysqldump.

chapters.title: Replication & High Availability
chapters.description: Master-slave replication, failover, connection pooling.

chapters.title: Auditing & Performance
chapters.description: Query optimization, EXPLAIN ANALYZE, index strategies, audit logs.

description: An introduction to cloud computing concepts, services, and architectures. Students will explore the major cloud providers (AWS, Azure, GCP), understand service models (IaaS, PaaS, SaaS), and deploy real applications to the cloud.
objectives: Understand cloud computing models and deployment types
Navigate and use core AWS/Azure/GCP services
Deploy web applications to a cloud platform
Understand serverless computing and managed services
Apply cost optimization and cloud security basics

chapters.title: Cloud Computing Fundamentals
chapters.description: Definition, IaaS/PaaS/SaaS, public/private/hybrid cloud, benefits and trade-offs.

chapters.title: Virtualization & Containers
chapters.description: VMs, hypervisors, Docker basics, containerization vs virtualization.

chapters.title: Core Cloud Services
chapters.description: Compute (EC2/VM), storage (S3/Blob), databases (RDS), networking (VPC).

chapters.title: Serverless & Managed Services
chapters.description: AWS Lambda, API Gateway, managed Kubernetes, FaaS concepts.

chapters.title: Cloud Deployment
chapters.description: CI/CD pipelines, Infrastructure as Code (Terraform basics), deployment strategies.

chapters.title: Security in the Cloud
chapters.description: IAM, shared responsibility model, encryption at rest and in transit.

chapters.title: Cost & Performance
chapters.description: Pricing models, auto-scaling, monitoring with CloudWatch/Azure Monitor.

description: An advanced course covering the theory and practice of artificial intelligence and machine learning. Students will build upon their introduction to ML and explore deep learning, reinforcement learning, and the engineering challenges of deploying AI systems at scale.
objectives: Design and train deep neural networks using PyTorch or TensorFlow
Apply CNNs to image classification and object detection
Understand and implement sequence models (RNN, LSTM, Transformers)
Apply reinforcement learning to sequential decision problems
Deploy ML models as REST APIs

chapters.title: Deep Learning Foundations
chapters.description: Neural networks revisited, backpropagation, activation functions, optimization (Adam, SGD).

chapters.title: Convolutional Neural Networks
chapters.description: Conv layers, pooling, AlexNet, VGG, ResNet — image classification.

chapters.title: Transfer Learning
chapters.description: Fine-tuning pretrained models, feature extraction, Hugging Face models.

chapters.title: Sequence Models
chapters.description: RNNs, LSTMs, GRUs — text classification, time series.

chapters.title: Transformers & Attention
chapters.description: Self-attention, BERT, GPT architecture overview.

chapters.title: Reinforcement Learning
chapters.description: MDP, Q-learning, policy gradients, OpenAI Gym.

chapters.title: MLOps & Deployment
chapters.description: Model serialization, FastAPI serving, MLflow, monitoring drift.

description: An advanced cybersecurity course focused on system and infrastructure security. Students will conduct penetration testing, analyze malware, implement security controls, and understand incident response procedures. Hands-on labs in a sandboxed environment are central to this course.
objectives: Conduct structured penetration tests using industry methodologies
Analyze vulnerabilities in operating systems and applications
Understand malware behavior and reverse engineering basics
Implement SIEM and log analysis workflows
Lead and document an incident response process

chapters.title: Penetration Testing Methodology
chapters.description: Reconnaissance, scanning, exploitation, post-exploitation, reporting (PTES, OWASP).

chapters.title: Vulnerability Assessment
chapters.description: CVE database, CVSS scoring, Nessus, OpenVAS.

chapters.title: Exploitation Techniques
chapters.description: Buffer overflows, privilege escalation, Metasploit framework.

chapters.title: Malware Analysis
chapters.description: Static vs dynamic analysis, sandboxing, YARA rules.

chapters.title: SIEM & Log Analysis
chapters.description: ELK stack, log correlation, alert rules, threat hunting.

chapters.title: Incident Response
chapters.description: IR phases (preparation, identification, containment, eradication, recovery), forensics basics.

chapters.title: Red vs Blue Team Exercises
chapters.description: Simulated attacks and defenses in a lab environment.

description: A comprehensive course on building native and cross-platform mobile applications. Students will develop iOS and Android apps using React Native (or Flutter), covering UI design, device APIs, offline storage, push notifications, and App Store deployment.
objectives: Build cross-platform mobile apps with React Native or Flutter
Design mobile-first UIs following platform guidelines (HIG, Material Design)
Integrate device APIs: camera, GPS, accelerometer, notifications
Manage offline data with AsyncStorage or SQLite
Publish an app to the Google Play Store and Apple App Store

chapters.title: Mobile Development Overview
chapters.description: Native vs cross-platform, React Native vs Flutter, project setup.

chapters.title: UI Components & Navigation
chapters.description: Core components, StyleSheet, React Navigation (stack, tab, drawer).

chapters.title: State Management in Mobile
chapters.description: useState, useContext, Redux Toolkit for mobile.

chapters.title: Device APIs
chapters.description: Camera, geolocation, push notifications (Expo Notifications), permissions.

chapters.title: Offline Storage & Networking
chapters.description: AsyncStorage, SQLite, fetching APIs, offline-first patterns.

chapters.title: Authentication
chapters.description: JWT auth, OAuth/social login, secure storage of tokens.

chapters.title: Testing & Deployment
chapters.description: Jest, Detox for E2E, building APK/IPA, App Store submission.

description: An advanced course on cloud-native architecture and microservices. Students will design, build, and deploy distributed systems using Docker, Kubernetes, and service mesh technologies, applying patterns like API gateways, circuit breakers, and event-driven communication.
objectives: Design microservices architectures from monoliths
Containerize applications with Docker and orchestrate with Kubernetes
Implement inter-service communication (REST, gRPC, message queues)
Apply resilience patterns: circuit breaker, retry, rate limiting
Set up CI/CD pipelines for cloud-native deployment

chapters.title: Microservices Architecture
chapters.description: Monolith vs microservices, bounded contexts, DDD basics, trade-offs.

chapters.title: Docker Deep Dive
chapters.description: Dockerfile, multi-stage builds, Docker Compose, registries.

chapters.title: Kubernetes
chapters.description: Pods, Deployments, Services, Ingress, ConfigMaps, Secrets, Helm.

chapters.title: Inter-Service Communication
chapters.description: REST vs gRPC, API Gateway pattern, message brokers (Kafka, RabbitMQ).

chapters.title: Resilience Patterns
chapters.description: Circuit breaker (Hystrix/Resilience4j), retry, timeout, bulkhead.

chapters.title: Observability
chapters.description: Distributed tracing (Jaeger), metrics (Prometheus), logging (ELK).

chapters.title: CI/CD for Cloud-Native
chapters.description: GitHub Actions, GitOps, ArgoCD, blue-green and canary deployments.

description: A practical course on managing IT projects from inception to delivery. Students learn to apply both traditional (Waterfall) and agile (Scrum, Kanban) methodologies, manage risks, lead teams, and communicate with stakeholders effectively.
objectives: Understand and compare project management methodologies
Plan and track projects using Gantt charts and backlogs
Apply Scrum: sprints, retrospectives, standups, backlog refinement
Manage project risks, budgets, and stakeholder expectations
Lead a team project from requirements to delivery

chapters.title: Project Management Fundamentals
chapters.description: Project lifecycle, scope, time, cost triangle, stakeholder analysis.

chapters.title: Traditional Methods
chapters.description: Waterfall, V-model, WBS, Gantt charts, milestone planning.

chapters.title: Agile & Scrum
chapters.description: Agile manifesto, Scrum framework, sprints, ceremonies, artifacts.

chapters.title: Kanban & Lean
chapters.description: Kanban board, WIP limits, flow metrics, Lean principles.

chapters.title: Risk Management
chapters.description: Risk identification, probability-impact matrix, mitigation strategies.

chapters.title: Team Leadership & Communication
chapters.description: Roles and responsibilities, conflict resolution, client communication.

chapters.title: Project Delivery
chapters.description: UAT, release planning, retrospectives, post-mortem analysis.

description: An advanced course on Big Data technologies, distributed storage, and scalable processing frameworks. Students will work with Hadoop, Spark, and modern data lake architectures to process and analyze massive datasets.
objectives: Understand the Big Data ecosystem and its challenges (Volume, Velocity, Variety)
Process large datasets with Apache Spark (batch and streaming)
Design scalable data lake and data warehouse architectures
Apply ETL pipelines and data orchestration with Airflow
Visualize insights from large-scale data

chapters.title: Big Data Fundamentals
chapters.description: The 5 Vs, Hadoop ecosystem overview, HDFS, MapReduce concept.

chapters.title: Apache Spark
